evidence.finding: No uniqueItems constraint on rules array; JSON Schema cannot express semantic ordering constraints

security_implication: P1-F2 remains exploitable at schema layer

evidence.finding: No pattern constraint on target_glob to reject absolute paths starting with /

security_implication: P1-F3 remains exploitable at schema layer

evidence.finding: All examples follow deny-before-allow and repo-relative conventions, but this is not enforced by schema

security_implication: Convention provides no protection against malicious matrices

evidence.finding: Ajv validation documented as manual step (npx ajv validate), not automated runtime gate

security_implication: No automated enforcement of schema before matrix consumption

evidence.finding: File exists but is not imported anywhere in codebase except test file; grep for evaluatePreDispatchToolCalls found only definition and test usage

security_implication: Permissions gate not deployed; both P1s remain exploitable in production

evidence.finding: globMatches() accepts any glob against any target; no absolute-path rejection; since gate not integrated, no call-site mitigations exist

security_implication: No runtime mitigation for P1-F3 absolute-path escape

security_impact: Both P1s remain exploitable; no defense-in-depth at schema layer

investigation: Is there an open spec/issue tracking permissions-gate integration?
Should the gate be integrated into deep-loop dispatch?
What is the integration timeline if planned?
If not planned, why does the code exist?
rationale: Determine if we have a P0 deployment gap (security code written but not deployed) or if P1s are accepted risks pending future integration
